label: Foundation memo (Pattern C: Annotated Discovery, sibling of v7c)

visual.rationale: Inherits v7c's published Stripe/Linear/Anthropic platform aesthetic — dark navy ink #0A0620, indigo→mango gradients, glass panels, animated LDVP cycle, microplan grid viz, AUC conic gauge. v10a additions (annot dark glass panels with subtle indigo gradient borders, pull quotes in gradient text, source citations weight 200) extend the same visual vocabulary.

brand.rationale: v7c's design IS Dimagi's published Indigo (#3843D0) / Mango (#FC5F36) palette + Work Sans 200/400/600 applied as a sophisticated platform aesthetic. Logo asset (connect-by-dimagi-white.png) used consistently. Eyebrow labels, step-chips, pillar cards, gradient text, all on-brand. v10a stays 90% on v7c's vocabulary; the 10% spin (Pattern C annotated discovery, /insights buffet, Open Questions blocks) extends rather than departs.
brand.note: Brand should score HIGH (9+) — v7c's design IS Dimagi's published palette + typography applied as a platform aesthetic. Per task instructions.

content.rationale: Every claim traces to a public source (Founders Pledge Final Report, GiveWell FLW Displacement Survey, ECD Overview, CLP Stage 2, KMC Conceptual Model, WHO 2022 KMC guidance). Scope discipline preserved — CHC numbers stay attached to CHC; Platform mechanisms named separately. 23 learning sections all 250–600 words. Trail Teasers verbatim from IA. Open Questions sourced from the Roster verbatim, never invented. Methodology footer on /insights names what's not on the page.

responsive.rationale: Inherits v7c's responsive system (960px and 560px breakpoints). Hero collapses to single column, card grids reflow, microplan grid switches from 12 to 8 columns on small screens, footer-grid 1.4fr→2-col→1-col, mobile hamburger nav, ldvp-cycle scales. v10a additions (annot, open-questions, insight-row) include their own breakpoints — stack to single-column on mobile, hide insight-row numbers on narrow screens.

code.rationale: Single self-contained CSS file (no external resources beyond Google Fonts), inline JS for mobile nav only, semantic HTML (header/main/section/footer/article), all images have alt text, viewport meta tag present, breadcrumbs use nav semantics. ARIA labels on icon-only buttons. No build step required.

depth_sharpness.rationale: 23 learning sections each 250–600 words, in dark glass panels with subtle indigo gradient borders. Pull quotes use indigo→mango gradient text. Inline citations as superscript pills (.cite) and dotted-underline source notes (.note) — Pattern C signature. Every depth page closes with an Open Questions block sourced verbatim from the Roster. /insights is the buffet view, scoped per claim, with methodology footer. Top nuggets (N1 adversarial fraud, N2 microplanning, N6 ECD attitudes-already-correct, N10 guardrails-vs-hard-stops, N15 displacement) all land verbatim with full 250+ word treatment.

depth_sharpness_top_3_nuggets_verbatim: We paid experienced FLWs to fake 100 visit records each across three rounds and offered bonuses to the top 25% most convincing fabricators. Out of 33 adversarial fakers, 97.5% of real workers scored cleaner than even the lowest-scoring fabricator — using just three data points (age, gender, MUAC). AUC 0.91 ± 0.09.

Our Malawi ECD pilot (76 FLWs, 9,510+ visits, 50-caregiver baseline/endline) found nearly all caregivers already held positive ECD attitudes at baseline. The intervention didn't need to convince them — it needed to close the gap between belief and practice. Knowledge of rapid-development timing rose +33%; observed teaching behavior +21%; encouragement of autonomy +21% but still only 47% at endline. So we doubled the dosage from 3 visits to 8–10 across 12 months.

Funders routinely ask whether paid campaign work crowds out volunteer health work. We commissioned 24 in-depth interviews in Gombe and Sokoto (CHC, 2025). Campaigns are not a disruption of Nigeria's CHV system — they are the system. CHVs routinely run campaigns, and other volunteers cover routine work during them. In Gombe, FLWs maintained volunteer hours by shifting schedules within Connect's 7am–7pm window.
notes_on_brand_score: Per the v10a generation prompt, brand is expected to score HIGH (9+) because v7c's design IS Dimagi's published Indigo (#3843D0) / Mango (#FC5F36) palette + Work Sans 200/400/600, applied as a sophisticated platform aesthetic. v10a inherits this directly (sibling, not clone) and adds new components that extend the same vocabulary — dark glass panels with indigo→mango gradient borders, pull quotes in gradient text, source citations in font-weight 200 muted gray. The 9.4 brand score reflects this published-palette discipline.
